name: Kirtimukha

visual_attributes.morphology: swallowing fierce face with huge fangs and gaping mouth
distinctive_features: often only the upper jaw and top of the face visible
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: varies depending on temple architecture, often large enough to be a prominent feature above temple entrances
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: positioned above temple entrances or deity images, sometimes integrated into temple pinnacles

domain_specific_visual.APOTROPAIC_DEMON_MASK: gruesome, awe-inspiring guardian of the threshold
domain_specific_visual.GAVAKSHA_TOPPER: tops gavaksha (kudu, nasi) motifs in Dravidian architecture

domain_specific_functional.SYMBOL_OF_REABSORPTION: marks the entry into the temple, signifying reabsorption
domain_specific_functional.REPRESENTS_CONSUMPTION: engaged in swallowing, representing the 'all consuming'

relationships.description: Created by Shiva from a burst of power from his third eye.
Rahu was sent by Jalandhara to challenge Shiva, leading to the creation of Kirtimukha.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: serves as a protective symbol and representation of Shiva's power

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic demon face without swallowing action
